title: AN ACT ESTABLISHING A TEN-BED MUNICIPAL HOSPITAL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN JUAN, METRO MANILA, TO BE KNOWN AS THE SAN JUAN MUNICIPAL HOSPITAL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 2223 / 86 OG No. 23, 4008 (June 4, 1990)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 6882, March 08, 1990 ]

AN ACT ESTABLISHING A TEN-BED MUNICIPAL HOSPITAL IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF SAN JUAN, METRO MANILA, TO BE KNOWN AS THE SAN JUAN MUNICIPAL HOSPITAL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Title of Act

. 芒聙聰 This Act shall be known and cited as "An Act Establishing a Municipal Hospital in the Municipality of San Juan, Metro Manila."

SEC. 2.

Declaration of Policy.

芒聙聰 It is the policy of the State to secure the well-being of the people by providing them adequate health services.

SEC. 3.

Creation of the San Juan Municipal Hospital.

芒聙聰 There shall be established, under the direct supervision of the Director of Medical Services of the Department of Health, a ten-bed municipal hospital in the Municipality of San Juan, Metro Manila, to be known as the San Juan Municipal Hospital.

SEC. 4.

Appropriations.

芒聙聰 The amount necessary to carry out the provisions of this Act shall be charged against the appropriations authorized for the purpose under the Fiscal Year 1989 General Appropriations Act. Thereafter, such sums as may be necessary for the operation and maintenance of the herein hospital shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 5. Before the Construction of the hospital, the Secretary of Health shall issue a written assurance that personnel of the hospital will be appointed and that funds for its maintenance and operation have been appropriated.

SEC. 6.

Effectivity

. 芒聙聰 This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JOVITO R. SALONGA

(Sgd.) RAMON V. MITRA

President of the Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on December 22, 1989.

(Sgd.) EDWIN P. ACOBA

(Sgd.) QUIRINO D. ABAD SANTOS, JR.

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary of the House of Representatives

Approved: March 8, 1990

(Sgd.) CORAZON C. AQUINO

President of the Philippines
